figures.name: Dido (Elissa)
figures.title: Founder of Carthage
figures.summary: A Tyrian princess who fled a power struggle and founded Carthage, building it into a powerful maritime empire.

figures.name: Hannibal Barca
figures.title: Carthaginian General
figures.quote: Let us relieve the Romans from the anxiety they have so long experienced, since they think it tries their patience too much to wait for an old man's death.
figures.summary: One of history's greatest military commanders, who led an audacious invasion of Italy over the Alps during the Second Punic War.

figures.name: Massinissa
figures.title: The Cavalry King
figures.summary: A Numidian king who allied with Rome, providing decisive cavalry support at the Battle of Zama, and united Numidia.

figures.name: Jugurtha
figures.title: Guerrilla King of Numidia
figures.quote: Rome is a city for sale, and doomed to perish as soon as it finds a buyer.
figures.summary: A brilliant tactician who waged a guerrilla war against Rome, exposing its corruption before being captured.

figures.name: Juba II
figures.title: Scholar King of Mauretania
figures.summary: A Hellenistic scholar installed as a client king by Augustus, he brought a multicultural renaissance to North Africa.
